title: Paul Thomas Anderson Wins Best Director Oscar for 'One Battle After Another'
text: Paul Thomas Anderson won Best Director for "One Battle After Another," his first Oscar win in the category. PTA's Best Director win feels less like a surprise than a long-overdue surrender, as he had lost his previous 11 Oscar nominations—among the most of any living winless filmmaker. Anderson won the top prize at the Directors Guild of America Awards, historically one of the most reliable predictors for this Oscars race. IndieWire's chief film critic David Ehrlich awarded the movie an A, calling it "the first movie of its size to accurately crystallize how insane it feels to be alive right now." The race had been competitive against Ryan Coogler's "Sinners," but as the 2026 Oscars wrapped with both Paul Thomas Anderson and Ryan Coogler winning their first Academy Awards, more Best Director history seemed inbound—though ultimately it was Anderson who took home the directing award.
